questions.text: The SI unit of electric current is:
questions.explanation: Ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current. 1 A = 1 C/s (coulomb per second).

questions.text: Resistance is directly proportional to length of conductor.
questions.explanation: R ∝ l (length). Resistance increases with length. R ∝ 1/A (area). Resistance decreases with cross-sectional area.

questions.correctAnswer: V = IR, where V is potential difference, I is current, R is resistance.
questions.explanation: Ohm's law states that current through a conductor is directly proportional to potential difference across it, provided temperature remains constant.
Electric power P = VI = I²R = V²/R. Power is measured in watts (W).
